title: News Corp countersues Brave for allegedly 'scraping' articles for AI
excerpt: News Corp, facing a lawsuit by search engine Brave Software, has filed a countersuit accusing it of "flagrant theft" in distributing and selling versions of...
content_html:
By Jonathan Stempel

July 21 (Reuters) - News Corp, facing a lawsuit by search engine Brave Software, has filed a countersuit accusing it of "flagrant theft" in distributing and selling versions of ‌articles from the Wall Street Journal and New York Post to AI companies.

Brave had last ‌year preemptively sued the media giant, seeking a court declaration that its practices were legal. It sued after receiving a cease-and-desist letter ​from News Corp, which is led by the family of Rupert Murdoch.

In a Tuesday filing in the Oakland, California, federal court, News Corp said Brave's unauthorized "covert scraping" and resale of its copyrighted articles fall "nowhere near the bounds" of legally acceptable conduct known as fair use.

"The more content Brave copies and sells, the more revenue ‌it generates, and the less incentive ⁠AI companies have to negotiate licenses with the publishers who produced the content," the lawsuit said. "Brave profits while publishers are cut out."

News Corp is seeking an injunction ⁠and unspecified monetary damages, plus damages of up to $150,000 per infringement.

Brave and its lawyers did not immediately respond to requests for comment outside business hours.

The competing lawsuits are part of a wave of litigation pitting publishers against technology ​companies ​that want to use copyrighted content to support AI.

BRAVE ​ACCUSED NEWS CORP OF DISRUPTING AI

Brave sued News ‌Corp in March 2025, seeking a court declaration that bundling copyrighted articles that can be licensed and sold is not copyright infringement.

It filed a revised complaint in May 2026, following what News Corp called failed negotiations for a "fair, market-based agreement."

Brave has argued that its indexing of News Corp content to make it searchable, and providing users with snippets and "high-level summaries" of that content, amounted to fair use.

The San Francisco-based ‌company also accused News Corp of threatening to disrupt advances ​in generative AI, which it said many consider "the most important ​innovation so far this century."

BRAVE ACCUSED OF 'TACKY TECH ​TRAFFICKING'

News Corp Chief Executive Robert Thomson said in a statement that Brave's looting ‌of his company's content reflected "blatant disregard" for the ​damage to how information ​is disseminated.

"This era of tacky tech trafficking must come to an end if journalism is to have a sustainable future," Thomson said.

Brave has said it is the smallest of the three U.S.-based ​companies to operate independent search engines "at ‌scale."

Google dominates that market, followed by Microsoft, which operates Bing.

The New York Post, Dow Jones ​and News Corp's British and Australian operations are also defendants in Brave's lawsuit.

(Reporting by ​Jonathan Stempel in New York; Editing by Edwina Gibbs)

content_html_zh:
乔纳森·斯坦普尔

7 月 21 日（路透社）——新闻集团面临搜索引擎 Brave Software 的诉讼，已提起反诉，指控其向人工智能公司分发和销售《华尔街日报》和《纽约邮报》的文章版本，“公然盗窃”。

Brave 去年先发制人地起诉了这家媒体巨头，寻求法院宣布其做法合法。 该公司在收到鲁珀特·默多克家族领导的新闻集团发出的停止函后提起诉讼。

新闻集团在周二向加利福尼亚州奥克兰联邦法院提交的一份文件中表示，Brave 未经授权的“秘密抓取”和转售其受版权保护的文章“远远超出了被称为合理使用的法律可接受行为的范围”。

诉讼称：“Brave 复制和销售的内容越多，产生的收入就越多，人工智能公司与制作内容的出版商谈判许可的动力就越少。” “在出版商被排除在外的同时，还能获得丰厚的利润。”

新闻集团正在寻求禁令和未具体说明的金钱赔偿，以及每次侵权最高 15 万美元的赔偿。

Brave 及其律师没有在工作时间外立即回应置评请求。

这些相互竞争的诉讼是出版商与希望使用受版权保护的内容支持人工智能的科技公司之间的诉讼浪潮的一部分。

BRAVE 指控新闻集团扰乱人工智能

Brave 于 2025 年 3 月起诉新闻集团，寻求法庭声明捆绑可授权和销售的受版权保护的文章不构成版权侵权。

在新闻集团称“公平、基于市场的协议”谈判失败后，该公司于 2026 年 5 月提交了修订后的投诉。

Brave 辩称，其对新闻集团内容建立索引以使其可搜索，并向用户提供该内容的片段和“高级摘要”，这相当于合理使用。

这家总部位于旧金山的公司还指责新闻集团威胁要破坏生成人工智能的进步，并表示许多人认为这是“本世纪迄今为止最重要的创新”。

勇敢者被指控“俗气技术贩运”

新闻集团首席执行官罗伯特·汤姆森在一份声明中表示，Brave 对其公司内容的掠夺反映出“公然无视”对信息传播方式的损害。

汤姆森说：“如果新闻业要拥有可持续的未来，这个俗气的技术贩运时代就必须结束。”

Brave 表示，它是“大规模”运营独立搜索引擎的三家美国公司中规模最小的一家。

谷歌在该市场占据主导地位，其次是运营 Bing 的微软。

《纽约邮报》、道琼斯以及新闻集团的英国和澳大利亚业务也是 Brave 诉讼中的被告。

（乔纳森·斯坦普尔纽约报道；埃德温娜·吉布斯编辑）
